name: Current Affairs in Science & Technology (1)

sourceRefs.label: isro.gov.in/Wikipedia - SpaDeX mission details; Business Standard/Business Today - Gaganyaan IADT-01 test
First of the new 'Current Affairs' rotation track for Part A, opened after all 53 original syllabus-clause topics were completed (Day 53)

researchNotes: Opens the new Current-Affairs rotation track for Part A (decided 2026-06-27, after Part A's 53 original topics were exhausted). Unlike every other Part A topic so far, NO past-paper evidence can exist for events this recent — entirely web-research-sourced, citing specific dates/numbers rather than vague summaries, since 'recent space achievements' alone isn't a quizzable fact but 'SpaDeX docked on 16 January 2025' is.

reading.intro: Covers ISRO's most significant recent achievements: the SpaDeX docking mission (India's 4th-in-the-world space docking capability) and the Gaganyaan human spaceflight programme's progress toward its 2026 target.

qa.question: What is the SpaDeX mission, when was it launched, and what rocket was used?
qa.answer: SpaDeX (Space Docking Experiment) launched on 30 December 2024, using the PSLV-C60 rocket, deploying two small satellites (SDX01 and SDX02) into orbit to demonstrate autonomous docking technology.

qa.question: When did SpaDeX achieve its first successful docking, and what milestone did this mark for India?
qa.answer: 16 January 2025 — making India the 4th country in the world (after the USA, Russia, and China) to successfully demonstrate space docking technology.

qa.question: When was SpaDeX's SECOND docking demonstration achieved?
Why is SpaDeX's docking technology considered a precursor for Chandrayaan-4?
qa.answer: Because Chandrayaan-4's payload will be too heavy to launch in a single lift-off — it will require multiple launches and IN-ORBIT DOCKING (the exact capability SpaDeX demonstrated) to assemble the full mission.

qa.question: What is Gaganyaan, and what is its current target year (revised from an earlier date)?
qa.answer: Gaganyaan is ISRO's human spaceflight programme, aiming to send Indian astronauts into orbit. Originally planned for 2024, its target has been pushed to 2026.

qa.question: What was the IADT-01 test (24 August 2025), and what did it involve?
qa.answer: ISRO's first Integrated Air Drop Test for Gaganyaan — a dummy crew capsule weighing about 4.8 tonnes was dropped from roughly 3 km altitude by an IAF Chinook helicopter, descending safely via a sequenced parachute deployment before recovery.

qa.question: What is Gaganyaan's most recently revised target launch year, updating the earlier 2026 target?
qa.answer: As of early 2026, ISRO Chairman V. Narayanan set Gaganyaan's target at 2027 — a further revision from the 2026 target noted in earlier reporting, reflecting the programme's continued rigorous testing schedule.

qa.question: What key Gaganyaan test milestone was completed on 19 February 2026?
qa.answer: ISRO and DRDO conducted the final qualification-level load test at TBRL, Chandigarh — evaluating high-speed aerodynamic loads and changes in the crew capsule's ballistic trajectory.

qa.question: What is Chandrayaan-4's mission objective, and its current target year?
qa.answer: A lunar SAMPLE RETURN mission from the Moon's south pole, currently targeted for 2028 — a significant advancement beyond Chandrayaan-3's landing-only mission, and directly building on SpaDeX's docking capability for multi-launch assembly.
